prompt:
Recommend the cheapest-CAPABLE orchestrator (engine / provider / model-class /
effort) for Session 2 of 2 of Set 093 in a VS Code extension repo. Output a
single decisive recommendation with a 2-3 sentence rationale — not a survey.

Available seats: claude-code/anthropic (Opus-class or Fable-class),
github-copilot/openai (gpt-5.x coding-class), gemini. Effort: low/medium/high.

Set 093 = "Work Explorer module-row interactions". Session 1 (JUST COMPLETED by
claude-code/anthropic/opus/high) added persistent semantic `Plan` / `Session
sets` child nodes to every module row in the tree (protocol + pure model
derivation + webview 4-level ARIA rendering + Layer 2/3 pins).

Session 2 of 2 scope: a hover/focus-revealed inline ACTION STRIP on each module
row (AI Plan / Import Plan / Open Plan / AI Sets), mirrored in a right-click
context menu and the Command Palette; retire the click-path module QuickPicks
while keeping palette QuickPicks; thread an explicit module target through the
shared `pickModuleForAuthoring` seam so row/context invocations carry their
module (targeting parity across row vs context vs palette); an `Assign legacy
sets to module...` flow on Unassigned that stamps `module:` via a
format-preserving writer; targeting-parity + assign-stamping tests; a live
dogfood against a scratch multi-module repo; Layer 3 strip-visibility smoke.
It touches TS host + JS webview + package.json menus/commands + several wizard
authoring modules, and is the set-terminal session (change-log, Step 9 review,
advisory path-aware critique).

Which seat is cheapest-capable for Session 2, and why?
content:
### Recommendation

**claude-code/anthropic/opus/medium**

### Rationale

The cheapest-capable orchestrator is Opus at a medium effort level because it already holds the entire working context from the just-completed Session 1. This established context dramatically reduces the cost of the most complex Session 2 tasks: the cross-cutting refactor of the `pickModuleForAuthoring` seam and the final path-aware critique. Leveraging the existing "warmed-up" state avoids the significant ramp-up effort and token cost required to bring a new model class up to speed, making this the most efficient path to completion.
